paper_title: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators Rather Than Generators

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces replaced token detection, a discriminative pre-training task where a model learns to distinguish real input tokens from plausible synthetic replacements generated by a small generator network, improving efficiency and performance over masked language modeling
raw_extraction.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering
raw_extraction.research_problem: Computational inefficiency and performance limitations of masked language modeling in pre-training language representations
raw_extraction.extension: Uses a shared embedding strategy between generator and discriminator, with a small generator that produces plausible token replacements
raw_extraction.paper_title: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators Rather Than Generators

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces replaced token detection, a discriminative pre-training task where a model learns to distinguish real input tokens from plausible synthetic replacements generated by a small generator network, improving efficiency and performance over masked language modeling
raw_extraction.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering
raw_extraction.research_problem: Computational inefficiency and performance limitations of masked language modeling in pre-training language representations
raw_extraction.extension: Uses a shared embedding strategy between generator and discriminator, with a small generator that produces plausible token replacements
raw_extraction.paper_title: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators Rather Than Generators

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces replaced token detection, a discriminative pre-training task where a model learns to distinguish real input tokens from plausible synthetic replacements generated by a small generator network, improving efficiency and performance over masked language modeling
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: XLNet dataset (extended from Wikipedia and BooksCorpus with ClueWeb, CommonCrawl, Gigaword)
raw_extraction.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering
raw_extraction.research_problem: Computational inefficiency and performance limitations of masked language modeling in pre-training language representations
raw_extraction.extension: Trained on extended datasets (XLNet data) and for longer durations, achieving superior performance on downstream benchmarks with reduced computational cost
raw_extraction.paper_title: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators Rather Than Generators
